findings.issue: anySignal() attaches an `abort` listener to each caller-provided signal with `{ once: true }`, but `once` only auto-removes the listener AFTER the abort event fires. On the normal (non-aborted) request path the listener and its closure over the internal `controller` are never detached. makeRequest's `finally` clears the timeout but never removes these listeners. When a caller passes a long-lived signal (e.g. a component-scoped AbortController that outlives the request), each request leaks a listener until that signal aborts or is GC'd.
findings.remediation: In makeRequest's finally, remove the abort listeners from the caller signals (track the handler refs), or prefer AbortSignal.any() where available and abort the combined controller on completion.

findings.issue: makeRequest auto-injects `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` from localStorage whenever the caller has not set an Authorization header. oauthApi.init() and oauthApi.redeem() are pre-auth endpoints that do not set an Authorization header, so if a stale access_token is present in localStorage (e.g. an already-authenticated user triggering re-login or the invitation flow), the stale bearer token is sent to the init/redeem endpoints. acceptInvitation correctly sets its own Authorization header and is unaffected.
findings.remediation: Have oauthApi.init/oauthApi.redeem pass an explicit `Authorization: ''` (or a sentinel) to suppress auto-injection, or scope makeRequest's auto-injection to a whitelist of authenticated endpoints.

findings.issue: The app is wrapped in <React.StrictMode> (main.tsx). In dev, React double-invokes effects on mount: the effect runs, cleanup aborts, then the effect runs again. OAuthCallbackPage.completeOAuth and InvitationCompletePage.completeInvitation will therefore issue oauthApi.redeem / acceptInvitation twice in development. The AbortController cleanup prevents the first result from being applied, but the first fetch is still sent to the backend; if the handoff is single-use the second redeem can fail. Production is unaffected (no StrictMode double-invoke), so this surfaces as a dev-only flake/confusion.
findings.remediation: Guard the effect with a `hasFetchedRef` (useRef) so the network call is issued only once per mount even under StrictMode, while still aborting on unmount.

findings.issue: There is no unit test for the lib/api/utils module. anySignal() (multi-signal combination, pre-aborted short-circuit, reason propagation) and makeRequest's 30s timeout / anySignal wiring are exercised only indirectly via oauth.test.ts (which never asserts a signal is forwarded) and AppRouter integration tests. Timeout and signal-combination regressions would not be caught by a focused test.
findings.remediation: Add src/lib/api/utils.test.ts covering anySignal first-wins + reason propagation, and makeRequest timeout-aborts + forwards caller signal via anySignal.
